plan.analysis: The current query selector uses a mixed predicate that forces the explicit-user path through the system backlog. This violates the required scope-pure query lanes and causes the test to fail. The solution is to split the query into two separate lanes for user and system alerts, then merge and deduplicate in memory under a global cap.

contract_coverage.postcondition: Do not introduce or retain any shortcut explicitly prohibited by this invariant: Scope-asymmetric candidate selection keeps query execution and selection policy separate: the orchestrator issues one locally bounded explicit-user query and one locally bounded system-NULL query, then deduplicates candidate identity and applies one mode-aware global order and limit in memory. A mixed OR predicate, lane concatenation, provider mutation, or limiting before both lanes participate is not equivalent. Nonpositive limits perform no query; recent ordering normalizes naive and aware event times with stable id ties, while non-recent mode orders by id.

Scope-asymmetric candidate selection keeps query execution and selection policy separate: the orchestrator issues one locally bounded explicit-user query and one locally bounded system-NULL query, then deduplicates candidate identity and applies one mode-aware global order and limit in memory. A mixed OR predicate, lane concatenation, provider mutation, or limiting before both lanes participate is not equivalent. Nonpositive limits perform no query; recent ordering normalizes naive and aware event times with stable id ties, while non-recent mode orders by id.
The query should split into two scope-pure lanes and merge under a global cap.

files.algorithm: Split the query into two lanes: one for user alerts and one for system alerts. Merge and deduplicate in memory under a global cap.
files.description: Replace the mixed predicate with two scope-pure query lanes for user and system alerts.

plan.notes: The repair must ensure that the query is split into two scope-pure lanes and merged under a global cap. This will prevent the mixed predicate from forcing the explicit-user path through the system backlog.
